name: CSharpBestPracticesRoslynMapped
description: A Roslyn-oriented C# best-practices ruleset with detection metadata, analyzer overlap, diff signals, examples, and suggested fixes.

schema.title: human-readable rule name
schema.examples: bad and good examples where useful
schema.roslyn: syntax kinds, symbol kinds, and analysis requirements
schema.microsoft_analyzer_overlap: related Microsoft analyzer rule IDs where known or closely overlapping

rules.title: Use PascalCase for public types and members
rules.intent: Keep public API names consistent with .NET naming conventions.
rules.guidance: Flag public classes, structs, records, interfaces, enums, methods, properties, events, and fields whose names do not follow PascalCase.

examples.bad: public class customerService { public void processOrder() {} }
examples.good: public class CustomerService { public void ProcessOrder() {} }

rules.suggested_fix: Rename the public symbol using PascalCase.

rules.title: Use camelCase for parameters and local variables
rules.intent: Make local scope identifiers visually distinct from public API members.
rules.guidance: Flag parameter and local variable names that use PascalCase or unclear casing unless they are constants or intentionally named discards.

examples.bad: public void SaveOrder(int OrderId) { var TotalAmount = 10; }
examples.good: public void SaveOrder(int orderId) { var totalAmount = 10; }

rules.suggested_fix: Rename the parameter or local variable using camelCase.

rules.title: Prefix interfaces with I
rules.intent: Follow established .NET interface naming conventions.
rules.guidance: Flag interface declarations that do not begin with a capital I followed by another capital letter.
rules.suggested_fix: Rename the interface using the IName convention.

rules.title: Avoid vague method names
rules.intent: Reduce ambiguity and make behavior easier to understand.
rules.guidance: Flag overly generic method names such as Process, Handle, Execute, DoWork, Run, or Manage when the method body or containing type suggests a more specific action.
rules.suggested_fix: Rename the method to describe the actual operation or outcome.

rules.title: Prefer early returns over deeply nested conditionals
rules.intent: Reduce cognitive complexity and make guard conditions explicit.
rules.guidance: Flag newly added or modified methods with nested if statements that could be flattened using guard clauses.
rules.suggested_fix: Invert guard conditions and return early where this preserves behavior.

rules.title: Always use braces for control statements
rules.intent: Prevent bugs when future statements are added.
rules.guidance: Flag if, else, for, foreach, while, and using statements whose embedded statement is not a block.
rules.suggested_fix: Wrap the embedded statement in braces.

rules.title: Limit method size and responsibility
rules.intent: Large methods are harder to review, test, and reason about.
rules.guidance: Flag added or modified methods that exceed configured thresholds for statement count, line count, or control-flow complexity.
rules.suggested_fix: Extract smaller private methods around cohesive behavior.

rules.title: Do not swallow exceptions silently
rules.intent: Silent exception handling hides failures and can cause data corruption or operational blind spots.
rules.guidance: Flag catch blocks with empty bodies or only comments, especially in modified code.

examples.good: try { Save(); } catch (Exception ex) { logger.LogError(ex, "Save failed"); throw; }

rules.suggested_fix: Log the exception, handle it explicitly, or rethrow it.

rules.title: Preserve stack trace when rethrowing
rules.intent: Keep original failure location available for debugging.
rules.guidance: Flag catch blocks that rethrow using throw ex instead of throw.

signals: ThrowStatement has expression matching catch variable

rules.suggested_fix: Replace throw ex with throw.

rules.title: Avoid catching general Exception unless justified
rules.intent: Broad catches often hide specific failure modes and make recovery unclear.
rules.guidance: Flag catch (Exception) or catch without a type when the block does not rethrow, wrap, or clearly handle the failure.

examples.good: catch (SqlException ex) { logger.LogError(ex, "Database save failed"); return false; }

rules.suggested_fix: Catch the most specific exception type you can handle meaningfully.

rules.title: Do not use exceptions for normal control flow
rules.intent: Exceptions are expensive and make expected paths harder to understand.
rules.guidance: Flag try/catch patterns around predictable lookup, parsing, or conversion operations where a Try* API exists.

signals: try contains Parse or dictionary indexer

rules.suggested_fix: Use TryParse, TryGetValue, or explicit condition checks for expected cases.

rules.title: Avoid blocking async calls
rules.intent: Prevent deadlocks, thread pool starvation, and poor scalability.
rules.guidance: Flag .Result, .Wait(), and GetAwaiter().GetResult() on Task or ValueTask in async-capable contexts.
rules.suggested_fix: Await the task instead of blocking on it.

rules.title: Avoid async void except event handlers
rules.intent: Async void exceptions cannot be awaited or observed reliably.
rules.guidance: Flag async void methods unless they are event handlers.

examples.bad: public async void LoadDataAsync() { await Load(); }
examples.good: public async Task LoadDataAsync() { await Load(); }

rules.suggested_fix: Return Task instead of void unless the method is an event handler.

rules.title: Pass CancellationToken through async call chains
rules.intent: Allow callers to cancel long-running or I/O-bound operations.
rules.guidance: Flag async methods that accept CancellationToken but fail to pass it to awaited cancellable operations.

signals: awaited invocation has overload with CancellationToken

rules.suggested_fix: Pass the available CancellationToken to cancellable APIs.

rules.title: Prefer Any() over Count() > 0
rules.intent: Avoid unnecessary enumeration and express intent clearly.
rules.guidance: Flag Count() > 0 or Count() != 0 on IEnumerable<T> where Any() is more appropriate.
rules.suggested_fix: Replace Count() > 0 with Any().

rules.title: Avoid multiple enumeration of IEnumerable
rules.intent: Prevent duplicated work, repeated queries, and inconsistent results.
rules.guidance: Flag repeated enumeration of the same IEnumerable variable in the same scope when the source is not known to be materialized.

examples.bad:
var count = items.Count();
var first = items.FirstOrDefault();

signals: same IEnumerable symbol enumerated multiple times

rules.suggested_fix: Materialize the sequence once or change the parameter type if multiple enumeration is expected.

rules.title: Use StringBuilder or string.Create for repeated string construction
rules.intent: Avoid repeated string allocations in loops.
rules.guidance: Flag string concatenation inside loops when the target variable is reassigned repeatedly.

examples.bad: foreach (var item in items) { text += item.Name; }

rules.suggested_fix: Use StringBuilder for repeated concatenation or string.Create for advanced allocation-sensitive scenarios.

rules.title: Avoid unnecessary ToList or ToArray materialization
rules.intent: Prevent avoidable allocations and memory pressure.
rules.guidance: Flag ToList or ToArray immediately followed by simple enumeration when materialization is not required.
rules.suggested_fix: Remove ToList or ToArray unless a snapshot is required.

rules.title: Dispose IDisposable instances
rules.intent: Release unmanaged resources and prevent leaks.
rules.guidance: Flag local disposable objects created with new that are not wrapped in using, using declaration, or disposed.
rules.suggested_fix: Wrap the disposable object in a using statement or using declaration.

rules.title: Use parameterized SQL queries
rules.intent: Prevent SQL injection vulnerabilities.
rules.guidance: Flag SQL command text built using string concatenation, interpolation, or format strings with external values.

examples.bad: var sql = "SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Id = " + userId;
examples.good: var sql = "SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Id = @userId";

rules.suggested_fix: Use DbParameter, Dapper parameters, EF Core LINQ, or another parameterized API.

rules.title: Do not log secrets or sensitive data
rules.intent: Protect credentials, tokens, PII, and regulated data.
rules.guidance: Flag logging calls that include suspicious names such as password, secret, token, apiKey, ssn, authorization, or raw request bodies.

signals: argument name or message contains sensitive keyword

rules.suggested_fix: Remove sensitive values from logs or replace them with safe identifiers.

rules.title: Return empty collections instead of null
rules.intent: Reduce null checks and prevent NullReferenceException.
rules.guidance: Flag methods returning collection types that explicitly return null.
rules.suggested_fix: Return Array.Empty<T>(), Enumerable.Empty<T>(), or an empty collection.

rules.title: Do not expose mutable collection properties directly
rules.intent: Protect object invariants and avoid uncontrolled mutation.
rules.guidance: Flag public properties returning List<T>, Dictionary<TKey,TValue>, or mutable collection interfaces.
rules.suggested_fix: Expose IReadOnlyList, IReadOnlyCollection, or a defensive copy.

rules.title: Depend on abstractions instead of concrete infrastructure
rules.intent: Improve testability and reduce coupling.
rules.guidance: Flag constructors that directly instantiate infrastructure dependencies instead of accepting abstractions.

examples.bad: private readonly SqlRepository repo = new SqlRepository();

signals: new concrete dependency inside service class

rules.suggested_fix: Inject an abstraction through the constructor.

rules.title: Avoid hardcoded configuration values
rules.intent: Allow behavior to vary by environment without code changes.
rules.guidance: Flag hardcoded URLs, connection strings, file paths, magic numbers, credentials, and environment names in application logic.
rules.suggested_fix: Move environment-specific values into configuration and validate them at startup.

rules.title: Add or update tests when behavior changes
rules.intent: Behavioral changes should be validated by automated tests.
rules.guidance: For diffs that change branching, return values, exception behavior, validation, parsing, or public API behavior, flag when no related test file changed.
rules.suggested_fix: Add or update tests that prove the intended behavior change and protect important edge cases.

rules.title: Use structured logging instead of string concatenation
rules.intent: Preserve queryable log fields and avoid unnecessary formatting work.
rules.guidance: Flag ILogger calls that concatenate strings or use interpolation instead of message templates.

signals: message argument is interpolated string or concatenation

rules.suggested_fix: Use a static message template and pass values as separate arguments.

rules.title: Avoid adding null-forgiving operators without justification
rules.intent: The null-forgiving operator suppresses compiler safety and can hide real null bugs.
rules.guidance: Flag newly added ! operators on nullable references, especially in production code.

examples.good:
if (user == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(user));
var name = user.Name;

rules.suggested_fix: Add an explicit null check or redesign the flow so the compiler can prove non-null.

rules.title: Validate public method arguments
rules.intent: Fail fast when callers pass invalid inputs.
rules.guidance: Flag public methods that dereference reference-type parameters without prior null validation when nullable annotations indicate they may be null.

examples.good: public void Save(Order order) { ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(order); repository.Save(order.Id); }

rules.suggested_fix: Validate required reference parameters with ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull.

rules.title: Avoid boolean flag parameters that change behavior
rules.intent: Boolean flags often hide multiple responsibilities and make call sites unclear.
rules.guidance: Flag methods with boolean parameters used to choose significantly different branches.
rules.suggested_fix: Split into intention-revealing methods or replace the boolean with a meaningful options type.
